title: Support ##0 cycle delays (#4263)
body:
## Summary

This patch adds support for `##0` cycle delays as defined in IEEE 1800-2023 14.11. Fixes #4263.

Per the spec, `##0` is treated specially depending on context:
- **Procedural**: if the default clocking event has not yet occurred in the current time step, suspend until it fires; otherwise continue without suspension.
- **Synchronous drive RHS**: no effect, as if not present.
- **Property/sequence**: zero-tick concatenation, removed as no-op.



## Reproducer

```systemverilog
module t;
  logic clk = 0;
  always #5 clk = ~clk;
  default clocking cb @(posedge clk);
  endclocking
  initial begin
    ##0;
    $display("time=%0t", $time);  // expected: 5
    $finish;
  end
endmodule
```

On master:

```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: Unsupported: ##0 delays
```

## Changes

- `V3AssertPre.cpp` (`visit(AstCycleDly*)`): Replace `E_UNSUPPORTED` for `##0` with three-way dispatch: remove as no-op in synchronous drive (`m_inSynchDrive`) and property/sequence (`m_inPExpr`) contexts; for procedural context, transform to `if (!event.isTriggered()) @(event);` using the default clocking block's `VlEvent`.
- `V3AssertPre.cpp` (`visit(AstNodeModule*)`): Add `m_defaultClkEvtVarp` state variable. Pre-create and cache the default clocking event var via `ensureEventp()` before `iterateChildren`, preventing an orphaned var after the clocking node is deleted during traversal.
- `t_clocking_unsup2.py`: Changed from fail-expected to passing lint test since `##0` is now supported.

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_clocking_zero_delay.{v,py}` -- covers all three `##0` contexts: procedural (wait-at-time-0, no-op-after-event, consecutive, mixed with `##1`), synchronous drive (comparative with/without `##0`), and property/sequence (one-cycle pulse with assertion action block).
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_clocking_unsup2.py` -- no longer expects failure.
- Removed `test_regress/t/t_clocking_unsup2.out` -- golden file for removed error.

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
